raw_consumer_statement:
I reported multiple unauthorized transactions on my credit card account to Barclays. The transactions occurred at several restaurants located in XXXX, a state where I have never traveled and where I was not physically present when the charges were made. 

After I reported the fraud, Barclays issued a replacement card, indicating that the card had been compromised. However, the bank later sent me a letter dated XX/XX/year> stating that they concluded the transactions were not fraudulent. 

The letter provides no explanation and no evidence supporting that determination. I have never visited the merchants listed on the transactions, never authorized those purchases, and received no benefit from them. 

I have now formally requested that Barclays provide the documentation and evidence used in their investigation, including receipts, authorization records, and transaction verification data. 

Despite this, Barclays is attempting to hold me responsible for these charges and has indicated that the balance may be reported to credit reporting agencies. 

I believe these charges are fraudulent and that the investigation conducted by the bank was inadequate and unsupported by evidence. I am requesting that Barclays remove the fraudulent charges from my account and provide documentation supporting any determination they claim to have made.
